item.title: Executive Order to Combat Cybercrime, Fraud, and Predatory Schemes Targeting American Citizens
item.description: This executive order articulates the federal government’s policy to address cybercrime, fraud, and predatory schemes that affect American households. It directs federal agencies to coordinate efforts to prevent, detect, disrupt, and prosecute these activities, with the aim of safeguarding citizens’ financial security and protecting the integrity of benefits and earnings. The order emphasizes interagency cooperation, information sharing, and potential regulatory or enforcement actions as part of a comprehensive approach to reducing losses from cyber-enabled crimes against American citizens. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Nominations Sent to the Senate for Federal Positions (USPS Governor, Ambassador at Large for Cyberspace and Digital Policy, and Assistant Secretary of Energy)
item.description: The White House announced nominations sent to the Senate for several federal roles. Jeffrey Brodsky, of Florida, is nominated to be Governor of the United States Postal Service for a term expiring December 8, 2029; Adam Cassady, of Virginia, is nominated to be Ambassador at Large for Cyberspace and Digital Policy; Kaveh Farzad, of Maryland, is nominated to be Assistant Secretary of Energy for International Affairs; William Gallo, of Florida, is nominated to be [position not specified in excerpt]. The posted excerpt also indicates that additional nominations are included in the full list. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Presidential Proclamation Imposing a Temporary Import Surcharge to Address Fundamental International Payments Problems
item.description: Presidential Proclamation issued by the President of the United States, invoking constitutional and statutory authority, including section 122 and section 301 of title 3, United States Code, and section 604, to impose a temporary import surcharge. The proclamation announces the government’s plan to levy a temporary surcharge on imports as a measure to address fundamental international payments problems, describes the intended duration and scope of the surcharge, and directs appropriate federal agencies to implement, administer, and enforce the surcharge and to report on its administration and impact. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Continuation of the Suspension of Duty-Free De Minimis Treatment for All Countries
item.description: This presidential action extends the policy that suspends the duty-free de minimis threshold for imports for all countries. It states that the President is continuing the suspension under authorities including the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974, thereby maintaining the current treatment of small-value imports from all countries. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Presidential Action to End Certain Tariff Actions
item.description: Under the authority vested in the President by the Constitution and federal law, including the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974, this action terminates specific tariff measures that had previously been imposed. The action designates which tariffs are affected, outlines the steps for implementing the termination, and specifies the timeframe or date by which the changes take effect. It directs applicable agencies to carry out the termination and to adjust regulatory and administrative processes accordingly, while leaving other tariff actions unchanged. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Executive Order to Promote National Defense by Ensuring an Adequate Domestic Supply of Elemental Phosphorus and Glyphosate-Based Herbicides
item.description: This presidential executive order, issued under constitutional authority and the Defense Production Act of 1950 (as amended), directs actions to secure the domestic supply of elemental phosphorus and glyphosate-based herbicides. It invokes authorities under 50 U.S.C. 4501 et seq. and section 301 of title 3, United States Code, and begins with a Section 1 titled 'Policy and Findings.' The measure aims to bolster national defense and agricultural resilience by ensuring an adequate domestic supply of these critical materials, outlining policy objectives and findings related to supply chain vulnerabilities and the need for governmental action to support production, procurement, and related activities. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Proclamation by the President Honoring George Washington on Washington's Birthday, 2026
item.description: A presidential proclamation issued in February 2026 commemorates George Washington's birthday, recognizing his leadership in the American Revolution and his role in founding the United States, and marking the 250th anniversary of American independence. The document describes Washington as a central figure in American history and identifies the occasion as a time for national remembrance. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.
